articles.title: 108yo and 103yo WWII veterans meet after more than 80 years

children.content: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

image.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

articles.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith speaks after Anzac Day event in Queensland

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith shook hands and posed for photos with fellow attendees after the service on the Gold Coast wrapped up.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith shakes hands with supporters in a crowd on a rainy day.

children.content: Australians paid tribute to those who have served at services and marches, but the solemn mood at Melbourne, Sydney and Perth Dawn Services was interrupted by boos during Welcome to Country addresses. Follow live.

items.title: Gallipoli service underway as Australians mark Anzac Day in solemn memorials

updates.title: The Melbourne man who confronted dawn service hecklers
'No-one wins a war': Veteran reflects on Anzac Day

items.title: The donkey marching for the memory of all animals that serve in war

children.content: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

image.altText: A grey donkey face up close in a paddock

items.title: ICAC investigating senior SA police officer for alleged misconduct

children.content: The state's anti-corruption agency says the officer, who cannot be named, is under investigation to determine whether they "committed a breach of discipline contrary to the SA Police Code of Conduct".

image.altText: A building with a piping shrike logo on it

items.title: 'Fear and hope' of foster care journey inspires teen artist

children.content: An Indigenous teenager is already making a name for herself as an artist, using her work to explore her identity and experiences in the care system.

image.altText: Keira creating an artwork at her kitchen table.

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care

children.content: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

image.altText: A woman with blue eyes and short blonde hair smiles. She is outside.

items.title: Bail denied for woman accused of repeatedly trying to kill ex-partner

children.content: The accused's lawyers applied for her release on bail last year on the basis that she had "dental and medical issues" that could not be managed in prison.

image.altText: A woman sitting at a desk in front of a bookcase

items.title: Councillor convicted of drink driving still able to attend Anzac Day parade

children.content: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

image.altText: a man smiles at the camera

items.title: Oyster growers enduring 'death by a thousand cuts' after being closed again

children.content: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

thumbnail.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

items.title: New Adelaide Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

image.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

items.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing

children.content: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

image.altText: Melbourne Demons' Kysaiah Pickett jumps into a Richmond player to take a mark in an AFL game.

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' leave camp in Syria months after failed attempt

children.content: A group of Australian families with ties to former Islamic State fighters have begun another attempt to travel home to Australia after spending years stranded in a Syrian refugee camp.

image.altText: A crowd of journalists and photographers surround a small group of women wearing hijabs and face masks.

items.title: White House seeks firing squad, electrocution, gas for federal executions

children.content: The US is one of very few Western nations still using the death penalty, although public support for capital punishment has declined among Americans.

image.altText: A middle-aged man looks sternly at reporters as he stands behind a lectern in front of the Justice Department seal.

articles.title: Israel's killing of journalists in Lebanon highlights the impunity with which it acts

children.content: According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the Israel Defense Forces have committed more targeted killings of journalists than any other government's military since 1992.

image.altText: Mourners hold images depicting journalist Amal Khalil, during her funeral in Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump says Iran's regime is 'fighting like cats and dogs'. This is the reality

children.content: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

image.altText: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei

children.content: It comes as fighting continues between Israel and militant group Hezbollah in southern Lebanon where an extended ceasefire is in place. Follow live.

items.title: Dissecting the art of apologising, from MAFS men to Tucker Carlson

children.content: There's almost something weaponised about the contemporary apology, and perversely the social media culture that aims to teach self-awareness and accountability might just be responsible for it.

children.content: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

items.title: James Valentine knew good radio and I was his wide-eyed apprentice

children.content: As an aspiring broadcaster, I covertly watched everything James Valentine did on air. He taught me how in the absurd minutia of our lives, we reveal ourselves to each other.

items.title: 'Generous gift': James Valentine's voluntary assisted dying legacy

children.content: For someone who revelled in celebrating humanity, one of James Valentine's final wishes was to make it publicly known his choice to access NSW's VAD laws.

items.title: From arrests to resignations, the Epstein fallout one year on from Giuffre's death

children.content: Virginia Giuffre didn't live to witness the full impact of the truths she brought forward, but the arrests, resignations and the unravelling of reputations came in the wake of her decision to speak.

items.title: Rob Reiner's son Jake describes 'nightmare' after parents' deaths

items.title: Inmates accused of sexual assault in prison continued to share cells, report finds

children.content: A Queensland Ombudsman report has found three instances where prison inmates accused of sexually assaulting cellmates have continued to share cells with other prisoners.

children.content: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

items.title: Leaked Pentagon email shows US wants to punish NATO allies over Iran war

children.content: The email suggests the US may review the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands and Spain's NATO membership as punishment over their stance on the Iran war.

image.altText: Falkland islanders celebrate referendum result.

items.title: Scrapped anti-whaling ship bought for $10 makes conservation comeback

children.content: The MV Steve Irwin anti-whaling vessel returns to marine conservation, restored to its former glory, after more than seven years out of action.

image.altText: a large camo painted ship sails through a harbour

items.title: Anzac biscuit-tin guitars sold to aid veteran charity

children.content: A Tasmanian guitar maker will donate Anzac biscuit-tin guitars to his local RSL to raise money for veteran mental health charity.

items.title: Tiny mountain possum bred at lower altitudes due to climate change

children.content: Trevor Evans has been breeding the endangered mountain pygmy possum to better deal with the effects of climate change. The population at his sanctuary has grown rapidly in the process.

image.altText: A Mountain Pygmy Possum

items.title: Family keeps century-old farming trade alive

children.content: Brothers in northern NSW continue a tool trade that's more than 100 years old. They believe they are some of the last manufacturers of ear-marking pliers in Australia.

items.title: Netanyahu delayed revealing prostate cancer diagnosis due to Iran war

items.title: 'Homophobia has no place': AFL boss rejects ruling on slurs in sport

items.title: Family history drives Wallaroos star in Anzac Day Test against NZ

children.content: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.
